topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the blunt finality of a closing door. "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise celebration for Sarah has been cancelled." I stared at the screen, my heart rate, which had been a frantic drum solo moments before, slowly settling into a steady, almost unnerving rhythm. A deep sigh escaped my lips, the tension that had been coiled tight in my shoulders finally unspooling. I leaned back in my chair, the worn fabric of the seat molding perfectly to my back, and let my gaze drift to the window, watching the mundane traffic flow below. A genuine smile, slow and warm, began to spread across my face, chasing away the lingering anxiety. It was just... a reprieve.

[story 2]

When I read the message, the air seemed to thin out of the room. For a full minute, I didn't move, just absorbing the information. Then, a sudden lightness washed over me, like stepping out of deep water into open air. I pushed my chair back, the scrape of metal against the floor sounding loud in the sudden quiet. I walked over to the kitchen counter, picking up a glass of water, taking a long, slow sip. My fingers unconsciously smoothed down the wrinkles on my shirt. A soft chuckle escaped my throat, a sound I hadn't realized I was holding back. The weight I hadn't known I was carrying just lifted, leaving a pleasant emptiness in its wake.

[story 3]

My eyes scanned the text multiple times, searching for a typo, a mistake, anything that suggested this was a prank. Nothing. Just the official notification of a no-party. A palpable sense of pressure that had been constricting my chest for days suddenly dissolved. I let out a breath so deep it felt like it purged everything stale. I stood up, stretching my arms high above my head, feeling every muscle in my body stretch and loosen. A slow, easy grin bloomed on my face, and I started humming a tuneless melody. The knot in my stomach, so tight and painful moments before, simply unraveled.
